item.title: Colour-Powder Water & Mude
item.dc:creator: hamza
item.content:encoded:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635427257859{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others. According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance.

Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Velellas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Gayetri Mantra Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Velellas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row el_class="sigma-shadow-1" Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Velellas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Sacred times and festivals According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance. According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]
item.excerpt:encoded: The holy temple of Sai baba was built in 1922, in the Shirdi town of Maharashtra. India.

css=".vc_custom_1635429780653{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]

Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Velellas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Sacred times and festivals According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance. According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]
item.excerpt:encoded: Shiva is the foremost deity in the Hindu pantheon and regarded as the destroyer.

!important;}"]Vibhishana, knowing that Ravana’s sin of abducting Sita is grievous, leaves him and goes to Rama. Kumbhakarna applauds Vibhishana’s decision. He says Vibhishana knows dharma, and such a one should be the king of Lanka. In the Mahabharata, we find Yudhishthira initially reluctant to wage war against his cousins. We Hindus believe that the Vedas transcend all time and don’t have a beginning or an end. The discourse that takes place between

history_list="%5B%7B%22history_title%22%3A%22Some%20Important%20Life%20Lessons%20From%20Bhagwat%20Gita%20%26%20Ramayana%22%2C%22history_content%22%3A%22The%20Bhagavad%20Gita%2C%20or%20the%20Gita%2C%20is%20the%20discourse%20that%20takes%20place%20between%20Lord%20Krishna%20and%20Arjun%20before%20the%20start%20of%20the%20Kurukshetra%20war.%20The%20teachings%20by%20Lord%20Krishna%20helped%20change%20Arjun%E2%80%99s%20perspective%20about%20life%20and%2C%20thus%2C%22%2C%22history_date%22%3A%22%3Cspan%3E2021%3C%2Fspan%3E%20Jun%2021%22%2C%22content_alignment%22%3A%22align-left%22%7D%2C%7B%22history_title%22%3A%22Hindu%20New%20Year%20%26%20Ramayana%20Avatara%20with%20Lord%20Shiva%22%2C%22history_content%22%3A%22The%20first%20major%20festival%20that%20we%20celebrate%20with%20the%20birth%20of%20the%20New%20Year%2C%20according%20to%20the%20Hindu%20almanac%2C%20is%20Sri%20Rama%20Navami.%20That%20is%20the%20avatara%20day%20of%20Rama.%20There%20is%20a%20Veda%20vakya%20that%20says%20Citra%20Paurnami%20marks%20the%20mukha%22%2C%22history_date%22%3A%22%3Cspan%3E2021%3C%2Fspan%3E%20Jun%2021%22%2C%22content_alignment%22%3A%22align-right%22%7D%2C%7B%22history_title%22%3A%22Lesson%20%26%20Greatness%20of%20the%20Vedas%20%22%2C%22history_content%22%3A%22In%20his%20Thirumandiram%2C%20Thirumoolar%20talks%20of%20the%20greatness%20of%20the%20Vedas%2C%20said%20R.%20Narayanan%20in%20a%20discourse.%20Thirumoolar%20says%20some%20talk%20of%20righteous%20things%20of%20dharma%20is%20subtle%20and%20hence%20difficult%20to%20understand%2C%20not%20mentioned%20in%20the%20Vedas.%22%2C%22history_date%22%3A%22%3Cspan%3E2021%3C%2Fspan%3E%20Jun%2021%22%2C%22content_alignment%22%3A%22align-left%22%7D%2C%7B%22history_title%22%3A%22Lord%20Krishna%E2%80%99s%20Birth%20In%20Unique%20Night%22%2C%22history_content%22%3A%22Andal%2C%20in%20Her%20Thiruppavai%2C%20refers%20to%20the%20night%20on%20which%20Krishna%20was%20born%20as%20%E2%80%9COr%20iravu%2C%E2%80%99%20meaning%20it%20was%20a%20unique%20night%20of%20dharma%20is%20subtle%20and%20hence%20difficult%20to%20understand.%20What%20made%20it%20unique%3F%20It%20was%20the%20night%20when%20the%20One%20without%20birth%20was%20born%2C%20elaborated%20V.S.%22%2C%22history_date%22%3A%22%3Cspan%3E2021%3C%2Fspan%3E%20Jun%2021%22%2C%22content_alignment%22%3A%22align-right%22%7D%5D"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row This holy shrine of Lord Vishnu forms a part of the four holiest sites (Char Dhams) in Hindu religion.[/vc_column_text][/vc_tta_section][vc_tta_section i_icon_fontawesome="fas fa-gopuram" add_icon="true" title="Somnath" tab_id="1603445224601-be89cb2b-53d5"][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635480994262{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"]This is one of the oldest pilgrimage centres in India and finds mention in the ancient books, like Shivpuran, Skandpuran and Shreemad Bhagvat. item.title: Story of Rama
item.dc:creator: jitendra
item.content:encoded:
The Mahabharata is the greatest exponent of dharma in practice. It is not easy to give a synonym for the term dharma, for in it is subsumed a highly complex subject. The way of dharma is subtle and hence difficult to understand. The History takes care to drive home the point that dharma has to be upheld for dharma’s sake alone and not for the sake of enjoyment of desires, pointed out Sri B. Sundar kumar The watchword in spiritual practice is that all jives are bound by karma and are redeemed with jnana.

Around 1500 B.C., the Indo-Aryan people migrated to the Indus Valley, and their language and culture blended with that of the indigenous people living in the region. Around 1500 B.C., the Indo-Aryan people migrated to the Indus Valley, and their language and culture blended with that of the indigenous people living in the region.

Indian Town "Bhubaneswar Is called city of temples"

The emphasis on gaining true jnana about the immortal nature of the soul as distinct from the body has to be internalised. The Gita echoes this truth about the ultimate value of jnana in freeing the jiva from the shackles of samsara. 

Lingaraj Temple was built by the King Jajati Keshari,

It was the ancient capital of Kalinga, and Dhauli near Bhubaneswar is the place where the famous battle was fought between Emperor Ashoka and the Kalinga army, after which Ashoka followed Buddhism. Mythological references and the epigraphic sources describe the area as Ekamra Kshetra and Saiva Pitha.

Temple of Lord Vishnu

The first sloka in the Yaksha Prasna has four questions and is followed by Yudhishtira’s succinct reply to all these. To the first question, “Who is it who makes Aditya or the sun to rise?”, the answer given is that the Vedas help Aditya to rise. Neelakanta in his commentary says that Yudhishtira thinks of the atma or the soul of man when sun is mentioned. So it means the soul which through the five senses, mind and intellect gains knowledge of the world.

The world famous temples 

  • Chhatarpur Temple
  • Ramakrishna temple
  • Lord Ram Temple
  • Lord Vishnu Temple

The Upanishads, the Bhagavad Gita, 18 Puranas, Ramayana and Mahabharata are also considered important texts in Hinduism.

BY Pandit S Dubey

Rameshwaram temple is quite famous and believed to be sacred by the people of Hindu religion all around the world because it is believed to be the first site where Lord Rama came with his wife Devi Sita after defeating Ravana. It is located on an island situated in Tamil Nadu, known as Rameshwaram. It is considered to be tremendously holy since it is one of the four sacred places in India or Char Dham.

Badrinath Temple is quite sacred among the Hindu people because it is believed to be one of the 108 temples created for Lord Vishnu in the world. It is located in Uttarakhand and is adorned by tourists of all religious beliefs. It is not only one of the four Dhams of the Char Dham pilgrimage in India, but it is quite beautiful as well. If you want to visit Badrinath, visit it between April to November because it will be impossible for you to visit the temple during winter due to the immense snow.

item.title: Shiva Shakti
item.dc:creator: hamza
item.content:encoded:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421476642{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]Building a temple, which belongs to whoever paid for it or to the community that occupies it, is believed to be a meritorious deed recommended to anyone desirous of heavenly reward. The choice of a site, which should be serene and lovely, is determined by astrology and divination as well as by its proximity to human dwellings. The size and artistic value of temples range widely, from small village shrines with simple statuettes to great temple-cities whose boundary walls, pierced by monumental gates (gopura), enclose various buildings, [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row content_placement="middle" css=".vc_custom_1633424878900{padding-top: 20px !important;padding-bottom: 20px !important;}" row_css_sm=".vc_custom_1633424878901{padding-top: 80px !important;padding-bottom: 80px !important;}"][vc_column width="1/2"][vc_single_image image="212" img_size="full" el_class="w-100" css=".vc_custom_1632836515677{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/2"][sigma_custom_heading style="style-2" title_element="h4" subtitle_element="p" heading_alighnment="text-left" title="Daily Pujas, Aarti & Temple Worship" subtitle="BENEFITS"][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421822782{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"]The morning and evening adorations (sandhya), being a very important duty of the traditional householder, are mainly Vedic in character but have become lengthy because of the addition of Puranic and Tantric elements. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421910525{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]The three main classes in the classic division of Indian society are the Brahmans, the warriors, and the commoners. The Brahmans, whatever their worldly avocations, claim to have by virtue of their birth the authority to teach the Veda, perform ritual sacrifices for others, and accept gifts and subsistence. The term alms is misleading; the dakshina offered at the end of a rite to a Brahman officiant is not a fee but an oblation through which the rite is made complete. [/vc_column_text][sigma_puja layout="grid" style="style-3" post_order="ASC" post_grid_responsive_xl="2" post_grid_responsive_lg="2" post_grid_responsive_md="2" post_grid_responsive_sm="1" post_per_page="2"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635422747589{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others. According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance.

Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Vellalas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Gayetri Mantra Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Vellalas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Sacred times and festivalsAccording to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance. According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others. item.content:encoded:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421476642{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]Building a temple, which belongs to whoever paid for it or to the community that occupies it, is believed to be a meritorious deed recommended to anyone desirous of heavenly reward. The choice of a site, which should be serene and lovely, is determined by astrology and divination as well as by its proximity to human dwellings. Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Velellas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Sacred times and festivals According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance. According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Velellas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Sacred times and festivals According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance. According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

item.title: Shri Harendra Pathak
item.dc:creator: hamza
item.content:encoded:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635505459747{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]Our mission is to share the Good of Hinduism, Loving, Faith and Serving. People ask questions related to Hinduism. Temple is a place where Hindu worship our Bhagwan Ram, Shiva, Vishnu, Krishna etc. galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book. It has survived not only five centuries, but also the leap into electronic typesetting Some Hindu teachers insist that believing in rebirth is necessary for living an ethical life. Their concern is that if there is no fear of karmic repercussions in future lifetimes.
Temple is a place where Hindu worship our Bhagwan Ram, Shiva, Vishnu, Krishna etc. Proin eget tortor industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type, People ask questions related to Hinduism. specimen book.Some Hindu teachers insist that believing in rebirth is necessary for living an ethical life. Their concern is that if there is no fear of karmic repercussions in future lifetimes,[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner content_placement="middle"][vc_column_inner width="1/2"][vc_custom_heading text="Faith" font_container="tag:h4|text_align:left|color:%23022147" use_theme_fonts="yes"][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635505367610{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"]we as humans naturally turn to a superhuman source of guidance, God. In Hinduism, the scriptures are heeded as the word of God. They are our encyclopaedia of rights and wrongs. The Swaminarayan mandir enhances one’s observance of dharma by providing an environment which facilitates observing these scriptural tenets. All of the devotees of the Swaminarayan mandir strive to strictly follow these tenets.
Secondly, these tenets are also emphasized in spiritual discourses regularly given at the mandir. Listening to these discourses would inspire one to imbibe these tenets as one understands their importance.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width="1/2"][sigma_progress_bar unit="%" bar_height="4px" bar_color="#7a0640" bar_background_color="#c62d2d" progress_bars="%5B%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22CHARITY%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2284%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22HELPED%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2270%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22DONATIONS%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2285%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22FOODS%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2292%22%7D%5D"][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row]

Their concern is that if there is no fear of karmic repercussions in future lifetimes,[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner content_placement="middle"][vc_column_inner width="1/2"][vc_custom_heading text="Faith" font_container="tag:h4|text_align:left|color:%23022147" use_theme_fonts="yes"][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635505367610{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"]we as humans naturally turn to a superhuman source of guidance, God. In Hinduism, the scriptures are heeded as the word of God. They are our encyclopaedia of rights and wrongs. The Swaminarayan mandir enhances one’s observance of dharma by providing an environment which facilitates observing these scriptural tenets. All of the devotees of the Swaminarayan mandir strive to strictly follow these tenets.
Secondly, these tenets are also emphasized in spiritual discourses regularly given at the mandir. Listening to these discourses would inspire one to imbibe these tenets as one understands their importance.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width="1/2"][sigma_progress_bar unit="%" bar_height="4px" bar_color="#7a0640" bar_background_color="#c62d2d" progress_bars="%5B%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22CHARITY%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2284%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22HELPED%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2270%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22DONATIONS%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2285%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22FOODS%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2292%22%7D%5D"][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row]

item.title: Santoshi Puja
item.dc:creator: hamza
item.content:encoded:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421476642{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]Building a temple, which belongs to whoever paid for it or to the community that occupies it, is believed to be a meritorious deed recommended to anyone desirous of heavenly reward. The choice of a site, which should be serene and lovely, is determined by astrology and divination as well as by its proximity to human dwellings. The size and artistic value of temples range widely, from small village shrines with simple statuettes to great temple-cities whose boundary walls, pierced by monumental gates (gopura), enclose various buildings, [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row content_placement="middle" css=".vc_custom_1633424878900{padding-top: 20px !important;padding-bottom: 20px !important;}" row_css_sm=".vc_custom_1633424878901{padding-top: 80px !important;padding-bottom: 80px !important;}"][vc_column width="1/2"][vc_single_image image="212" img_size="full" el_class="w-100" css=".vc_custom_1632836515677{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/2"][sigma_custom_heading style="style-2" title_element="h4" subtitle_element="p" heading_alighnment="text-left" title="Daily Pujas, Aarti & Temple Worship" subtitle="BENEFITS"][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421822782{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"]The morning and evening adorations (sandhya), being a very important duty of the traditional householder, are mainly Vedic in character but have become lengthy because of the addition of Puranic and Tantric elements. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635421910525{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]The three main classes in the classic division of Indian society are the Brahmans, the warriors, and the commoners. The Brahmans, whatever their worldly avocations, claim to have by virtue of their birth the authority to teach the Veda, perform ritual sacrifices for others, and accept gifts and subsistence. The term alms is misleading; the dakshina offered at the end of a rite to a Brahman officiant is not a fee but an oblation through which the rite is made complete. [/vc_column_text][sigma_puja layout="grid" style="style-3" post_order="ASC" post_grid_responsive_xl="2" post_grid_responsive_lg="2" post_grid_responsive_md="2" post_grid_responsive_sm="1" post_per_page="2"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635422747589{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others. According to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance.

Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Vellalas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Gayetri Mantra Yet despite the statements in the texts on dharma, there was considerable fluidity in the status of the castes. Communities such as the Vellalas, for instance, are regarded as Shudras by Brahmans but as a high caste by other groups. Sacred times and festivalsAccording to Hindu tradition, the Veda should not be studied in the presence of Shudras, but they may listen to the recitation of epics and Puranas. They are permitted to perform the five main acts of worship (without Vedic mantras) and undertake observances, but even today they maintain various ceremonies of their own, carried out without Brahmanic assistance. According to the texts on dharma, the duty of the fourth class (the Shudras) was to serve the others. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width="1/3"][vc_single_image image="3245" img_size="full" el_class="w-100"][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/3"][vc_single_image image="3296" img_size="full" el_class="w-100"][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/3"][vc_single_image image="3297" img_size="full" el_class="w-100"][/vc_column][/vc_row]

item.title: Shri Abhinash Sharma
item.dc:creator: hamza
item.content:encoded:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635505459747{margin-bottom: 20px !important;}"]Our mission is to share the Good of Hinduism, Loving, Faith and Serving. People ask questions related to Hinduism. Temple is a place where Hindu worship our Bhagwan Ram, Shiva, Vishnu, Krishna etc. galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book. It has survived not only five centuries, but also the leap into electronic typesetting Some Hindu teachers insist that believing in rebirth is necessary for living an ethical life. Their concern is that if there is no fear of karmic repercussions in future lifetimes.
Temple is a place where Hindu worship our Bhagwan Ram, Shiva, Vishnu, Krishna etc. Proin eget tortor industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type, People ask questions related to Hinduism. specimen book.Some Hindu teachers insist that believing in rebirth is necessary for living an ethical life. Their concern is that if there is no fear of karmic repercussions in future lifetimes,[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner content_placement="middle"][vc_column_inner width="1/2"][vc_custom_heading text="Faith" font_container="tag:h4|text_align:left|color:%23022147" use_theme_fonts="yes"][vc_column_text css=".vc_custom_1635505367610{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}"]we as humans naturally turn to a superhuman source of guidance, God. In Hinduism, the scriptures are heeded as the word of God. They are our encyclopaedia of rights and wrongs. The Swaminarayan mandir enhances one’s observance of dharma by providing an environment which facilitates observing these scriptural tenets. All of the devotees of the Swaminarayan mandir strive to strictly follow these tenets.
Secondly, these tenets are also emphasized in spiritual discourses regularly given at the mandir. Listening to these discourses would inspire one to imbibe these tenets as one understands their importance.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width="1/2"][sigma_progress_bar unit="%" bar_height="4px" bar_color="#7a0640" bar_background_color="#c62d2d" progress_bars="%5B%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22CHARITY%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2284%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22HELPED%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2270%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22DONATIONS%20%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2285%22%7D%2C%7B%22bar_label%22%3A%22FOODS%22%2C%22bar_value%22%3A%2292%22%7D%5D"][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row]
